metadata.title: CMMC 2.0 Level 2 Self-Assessment
metadata.description: Self-assessment of the SRE platform against CMMC 2.0 Level 2 requirements. CMMC Level 2 aligns with NIST SP 800-171 Rev 2, which contains 110 security requirements derived from NIST SP 800-53.
metadata.remarks: This self-assessment covers security requirements that are directly addressed by the SRE platform technical controls. Organizational and procedural controls (e.g., personnel security PS, physical protection PE, media protection MP beyond encryption) require additional policy documentation outside the scope of this platform assessment.

practices.title: Authorized Access Control
practices.implementation: Keycloak provides centralized identity management with group-based RBAC. Kubernetes RBAC scopes access to namespaces. User accounts are provisioned through Keycloak admin console or federated from enterprise AD/LDAP.

practices.title: Transaction and Function Control
practices.implementation: Istio AuthorizationPolicy CRDs enforce transaction-level access control based on source identity, HTTP method, and path. Kubernetes RBAC restricts API operations to authorized roles.

practices.title: Control CUI Flow
practices.implementation: Kubernetes NetworkPolicies enforce default-deny in all namespaces with explicit allow rules. Istio mTLS STRICT mode encrypts and authenticates all data flows. NeuVector provides network DLP to detect sensitive data in transit.

practices.title: Separation of Duties
practices.implementation: Keycloak groups map to distinct Kubernetes RBAC roles: platform-admins, developers, viewers, and auditors. Flux RBAC separates platform configuration from application deployment. Tenant namespaces isolate team resources.

practices.title: Least Privilege
practices.implementation: Pod security contexts enforce non-root execution, drop ALL capabilities, read-only root filesystem. RBAC roles scoped to individual namespaces. ServiceAccounts per workload with automountServiceAccountToken disabled. Kyverno policies enforce these requirements.

practices.title: Non-Privileged Account Use
practices.implementation: Kyverno policies enforce non-root container execution and block privileged containers. SSH access to nodes requires individual user accounts (no shared root). Keycloak enforces role-based access with separate privileged and non-privileged groups.

practices.title: Privileged Functions
practices.implementation: Kyverno disallow-privileged and disallow-privilege-escalation policies prevent non-privileged users from executing privileged functions. Kubernetes audit logging captures all privileged operations. Istio access logs record privileged API calls.

practices.title: Unsuccessful Logon Attempts
practices.implementation: Keycloak enforces brute force protection with configurable lockout thresholds, lockout duration, and IP-based rate limiting. Failed login attempts are logged and forwarded to Loki. Prometheus alerts on excessive failed authentication attempts.

practices.title: Privacy and Security Notices
practices.implementation: Keycloak login page can display privacy and security notices. SSH login banners are configured via Ansible os-hardening role. Additional notices on platform UIs require per-application configuration.

practices.title: Session Lock
practices.implementation: Keycloak enforces session timeouts with configurable idle and absolute session lifetimes. SSH sessions have idle timeouts configured via Ansible (ClientAliveInterval/ClientAliveCountMax). Grafana sessions expire based on Keycloak SSO session.

practices.title: Session Termination
practices.implementation: Keycloak automatically terminates sessions after configurable inactivity period. Users can manually terminate sessions via Keycloak account console. Administrator can revoke active sessions. SSH sessions terminated after inactivity timeout.

practices.title: Control Remote Access
practices.implementation: All management access through Keycloak SSO with MFA. Istio gateway provides TLS termination for all external access. SSH hardened via Ansible with key-only authentication, restricted ciphers, and session timeouts.

practices.title: Remote Access Confidentiality
practices.implementation: All remote access encrypted via TLS 1.2+ at the Istio gateway. SSH connections use FIPS-approved cryptographic algorithms. VPN or bastion host required for direct node access in production environments.

practices.title: Remote Access Routing
practices.implementation: All external access routes through the Istio ingress gateway as a managed access control point. No direct access to internal services from external networks. NetworkPolicies enforce traffic routing through approved paths.

practices.title: Privileged Remote Access
practices.implementation: Privileged remote access to management interfaces requires Keycloak MFA. SSH access to nodes requires key-based authentication with no password fallback. All privileged remote sessions are logged to Loki.

practices.title: Wireless Access Authorization
practices.implementation: The SRE platform is a software platform running on infrastructure. Wireless access control is the responsibility of the hosting environment's physical security controls.

practices.title: Wireless Access Protection
practices.implementation: Wireless access protection is the responsibility of the hosting environment's physical and network security controls.

practices.title: Mobile Device Connection
practices.implementation: Mobile device management is outside the scope of the SRE platform. Platform UIs accessed via mobile devices are protected by Keycloak SSO with MFA.

practices.title: Encrypt CUI on Mobile
practices.implementation: Mobile device encryption is outside the scope of the SRE platform.

practices.title: External Connections
practices.implementation: External system connections are controlled via Istio egress gateway and NetworkPolicies. Kyverno policies restrict egress destinations. NeuVector monitors and can block unauthorized external connections.

practices.title: Portable Storage
practices.implementation: Portable storage device control is outside the scope of the SRE platform and is the responsibility of the hosting environment.

practices.title: Control Public Information
practices.implementation: Istio AuthorizationPolicies and NetworkPolicies control which information is publicly accessible. Further organizational policy is required to define what constitutes public information.

practices.title: Role-Based Risk Awareness
practices.implementation: Platform documentation provides security awareness for platform administrators and developers. Formal role-based security awareness training program to be established.

practices.title: Role-Based Training
practices.implementation: Platform documentation and onboarding guides provide role-specific technical training. Formal cybersecurity training program to be established as an organizational control.

practices.title: Insider Threat Awareness
practices.implementation: NeuVector behavioral monitoring provides technical insider threat detection. Formal insider threat awareness training to be established as an organizational control.

practices.title: System Auditing
practices.implementation: Kubernetes API audit policy captures all authentication, authorization, and CRUD operations. Istio access logs capture all mesh traffic. OS auditd captures privileged operations. All events forwarded to Loki via Alloy.

practices.title: User Accountability
practices.implementation: Structured JSON logs include user identity (Keycloak subject, Kubernetes user, SPIFFE identity), timestamp, action, resource, and outcome. Correlation IDs enable tracing actions across components.

practices.title: Event Review
practices.implementation: Grafana dashboards provide audit event review with pre-built queries for authentication events, privileged operations, policy violations, and security incidents. LogQL enables ad-hoc investigation.

practices.title: Audit Failure Alerting
practices.implementation: Prometheus alerts on Loki ingestion failures, Alloy DaemonSet pod failures, disk pressure, and auditd service failures. AlertManager routes to configured receivers.

practices.title: Audit Correlation
practices.implementation: Grafana provides unified view of metrics (Prometheus), logs (Loki), and traces (Tempo) enabling correlation across data sources. Request IDs and trace IDs enable cross-component event correlation.

practices.title: Reduction and Reporting
practices.implementation: Loki provides on-demand audit log reduction via LogQL queries with filtering, aggregation, and pattern matching. Grafana dashboards generate compliance reports. Reports exportable as PDF/PNG.

practices.title: Authoritative Time Source
practices.implementation: NTP (chronyd) configured on all nodes via Ansible. All logs use UTC timestamps. NTP synchronization status monitored via Prometheus.

practices.title: Audit Protection
practices.implementation: Loki log storage encrypted at rest. RBAC restricts access to logging namespace. Retention policies prevent premature deletion. Kubernetes RBAC prevents tenant access to audit data.

practices.title: Audit Management
practices.implementation: Audit log management limited to platform-admin role via Keycloak group mapping. Logging namespace protected by NetworkPolicies and Kubernetes RBAC. Audit configuration managed exclusively via GitOps.

practices.title: System Baselining
practices.implementation: Git repository is the authoritative baseline. Flux CD reconciles cluster state continuously. All component versions pinned. Complete change history in Git.

practices.title: Security Configuration Enforcement
practices.implementation: Ansible STIG roles harden OS. RKE2 CIS profile hardens Kubernetes. Kyverno policies enforce workload configurations. Flux ensures configurations are not manually overridden.

practices.title: System Change Management
practices.implementation: All changes via Git PR workflow with branch protection, peer review, CI validation, and conventional commits. Flux automatically applies approved changes.

practices.title: Security Impact Analysis
practices.implementation: CI pipeline runs linting, policy tests, and compliance validation before merge. Flux provides dry-run capability (flux diff). Kyverno policy tests validate security impact of configuration changes.

practices.title: Access Restrictions for Change
practices.implementation: Branch protection requires peer review. Flux RBAC restricts reconciliation scope. Kyverno prevents direct kubectl modifications to platform resources. Keycloak RBAC limits change authority.

practices.title: Least Functionality
practices.implementation: Kyverno restricts capabilities, volumes, and host access. NeuVector blocks unauthorized processes. SELinux enforces mandatory access control. Minimal base images (distroless/Chainguard) reduce attack surface.

practices.title: Nonessential Functionality
practices.implementation: Container images use minimal base images with only essential packages. Kyverno blocks containers with unnecessary capabilities. NeuVector behavioral learning identifies and can block non-essential processes.

practices.title: Application Execution Policy
practices.implementation: Kyverno restrict-image-registries allows only approved Harbor registry. Kyverno verify-image-signatures enforces Cosign verification. NeuVector admission control provides additional image validation.

practices.title: User-Installed Software
practices.implementation: Kyverno restricts images to harbor.sre.internal only. Image signature verification prevents unsigned software. Tenants can only deploy to their authorized namespaces via GitOps.

practices.title: Identification
practices.implementation: Keycloak SSO with OIDC provides unique identification for all users. OIDC integration with Kubernetes API server maps Keycloak identities to Kubernetes subjects.

practices.title: Authentication
practices.implementation: Keycloak authenticates all users with configurable authentication flows. Supports password, MFA (TOTP, WebAuthn), Kerberos, and certificate-based authentication.

practices.title: Multifactor Authentication
practices.implementation: Keycloak enforces MFA (TOTP or WebAuthn) for all privileged access to platform management interfaces including Grafana, Harbor, NeuVector, and Kubernetes API.

practices.title: Replay-Resistant Authentication
practices.implementation: Keycloak OIDC tokens include nonce and expiration to prevent replay. Istio mTLS uses ephemeral session keys. SSH connections use challenge-response with key exchange.

practices.title: Identifier Management
practices.implementation: Keycloak manages user identifiers with unique usernames. Kubernetes ServiceAccounts provide workload identifiers. Istio SPIFFE provides cryptographic workload identity.

practices.title: Authenticator Management
practices.implementation: Keycloak password policies enforce complexity and expiration. cert-manager automates certificate rotation. OpenBao provides dynamic secret generation and rotation.

practices.title: Password Complexity
practices.implementation: Keycloak enforces password complexity: minimum length, character diversity (uppercase, lowercase, digits, special characters), and password history to prevent reuse.

practices.title: Password Reuse
practices.implementation: Keycloak maintains password history and prevents reuse of previously used passwords based on configurable history depth.

practices.title: Temporary Passwords
practices.implementation: Keycloak supports temporary passwords that require change on first login. Organizational procedures for temporary password distribution to be documented.

practices.title: Cryptographically-Protected Passwords
practices.implementation: Keycloak stores password hashes using PBKDF2. All authentication traffic encrypted via TLS. OpenBao secrets encrypted at rest and in transit.

practices.title: Obscure Feedback
practices.implementation: Keycloak login page obscures password entry. Platform UIs inherit Keycloak SSO behavior. Custom applications should be configured to follow this pattern.

practices.title: Incident Handling
practices.implementation: NeuVector alerts integrated into Prometheus/Grafana alerting pipeline. AlertManager routes incidents to configured receivers with linked runbooks for guided response.

practices.title: Incident Reporting
practices.implementation: Grafana dashboards provide exportable incident reports with timeline views and affected resources. Kyverno PolicyReports provide compliance violation history.

practices.title: Incident Response Testing
practices.implementation: NeuVector provides simulated attack detection capabilities. Formal incident response testing procedures to be documented and scheduled.

practices.title: Perform Maintenance
practices.implementation: Platform maintenance performed via GitOps (Flux CD). HelmRelease version updates are tracked in Git. Ansible playbooks provide OS-level maintenance with idempotent execution.

practices.title: System Maintenance Control
practices.implementation: All maintenance tools (kubectl, flux CLI, ansible) accessed via authenticated sessions. Tool versions pinned. Additional organizational controls for maintenance tool approval to be documented.

practices.title: Maintenance Tool Oversight
practices.implementation: Kubernetes audit logs capture all kubectl commands. Flux logs capture all reconciliation events. Additional organizational oversight procedures to be documented.

practices.title: Media Inspection
practices.implementation: All container images scanned by Harbor/Trivy before deployment. Image signatures verified by Kyverno. No external media introduced to the platform without scanning.

practices.title: Nonlocal Maintenance
practices.implementation: All maintenance performed remotely via authenticated, encrypted sessions (SSH with key auth, kubectl via Keycloak OIDC, Flux GitOps). MFA required for privileged maintenance.

practices.title: Maintenance Personnel
practices.implementation: Keycloak provides role-based access for maintenance personnel. Organizational policies for maintenance personnel authorization to be documented.

practices.title: Media Protection
practices.implementation: OpenBao access policies restrict secret retrieval to authorized workloads. Kubernetes Secrets encrypted at rest. Persistent volume data protected by storage-level encryption.

practices.title: Media Access
practices.implementation: Digital media access controlled via Kubernetes RBAC and OpenBao policies. Physical media access is the responsibility of the hosting environment.

practices.title: Media Sanitization
practices.implementation: Container image layers are immutable and garbage collected by Harbor. Persistent volume cleanup is handled by Kubernetes. Physical media sanitization is an organizational responsibility.

practices.title: Media Marking
practices.implementation: Physical media marking is outside the scope of the SRE platform.

practices.title: Media Accountability
practices.implementation: Harbor maintains a complete inventory of container images with SBOMs. Digital media tracking is automated. Physical media accountability is an organizational responsibility.

practices.title: Portable Storage Encryption
practices.implementation: Portable storage device encryption is outside the scope of the SRE platform.

practices.title: Removable Media
practices.implementation: Removable media restrictions are the responsibility of the hosting environment.

practices.title: Shared Media
practices.implementation: Shared media prohibitions are the responsibility of the hosting environment.

practices.title: Protect Backups
practices.implementation: Velero backups stored in S3-compatible storage with encryption at rest. Backup access controlled via RBAC and cloud IAM policies. Backup integrity verified via restore testing.

practices.title: Limit Physical Access
practices.implementation: Physical access control is the responsibility of the hosting environment (data center, cloud provider).

practices.title: Monitor Physical Facility
practices.implementation: Physical facility monitoring is the responsibility of the hosting environment.

practices.title: Escort Visitors
practices.implementation: Visitor escort is the responsibility of the hosting environment.

practices.title: Physical Access Logs
practices.implementation: Physical access logging is the responsibility of the hosting environment.

practices.title: Manage Physical Access
practices.implementation: Physical access management is the responsibility of the hosting environment.

practices.title: Alternative Work Sites
practices.implementation: Alternative work site controls are the responsibility of the organization.

practices.title: Screen Individuals
practices.implementation: Personnel screening is an organizational control. The platform supports access restrictions through Keycloak RBAC that can be applied after screening decisions.

practices.title: Personnel Actions
practices.implementation: Keycloak supports account deactivation and group removal for personnel transfers/terminations. Automated account deprovisioning can be configured via LDAP/AD federation sync.

practices.title: Risk Assessments
practices.implementation: Technical risk assessment is supported by NeuVector vulnerability scanning, Trivy image analysis, and Kyverno compliance reports. Formal organizational risk assessment process to be documented.

practices.title: Vulnerability Scanning
practices.implementation: Harbor + Trivy for image scanning on push. NeuVector for runtime vulnerability scanning. CIS benchmark scanning on all nodes. Kyverno prevents deployment of unscanned images.

practices.title: Vulnerability Remediation
practices.implementation: Trivy severity-based alerts trigger remediation workflows. Flux enables automated image updates. Ansible applies OS patches. Prometheus alerts on CRITICAL/HIGH CVEs.

practices.title: Security Control Assessment
practices.implementation: Kyverno PolicyReports provide continuous security control assessment. NeuVector CIS benchmarks validate configuration. OSCAL SSP documents control implementations. STIG validation scripts automate compliance checking.

practices.title: Plan of Action
practices.implementation: Kyverno background scanning identifies non-compliant resources requiring remediation. Formal POA&M tracking to be established as an organizational process.

practices.title: Security Control Monitoring
practices.implementation: Prometheus + Grafana for real-time metrics. NeuVector for runtime anomaly detection. Kyverno for continuous policy compliance. Loki for log analysis. All integrated into Grafana dashboards.

practices.title: System Security Plan
practices.implementation: OSCAL System Security Plan (SSP) documents all security control implementations. Control-to-component mapping maintained in machine-readable JSON format.

practices.title: Boundary Protection
practices.implementation: Istio ingress gateway provides single entry point. NetworkPolicies enforce default deny. NeuVector provides network microsegmentation. Istio egress gateway controls outbound traffic.

practices.title: Security Engineering
practices.implementation: Platform designed with defense-in-depth: multiple security layers (Istio mTLS, NetworkPolicies, Kyverno policies, NeuVector runtime security, RBAC). Security architecture documented in docs/architecture.md.

practices.title: Security Function Separation
practices.implementation: Namespaces isolate platform components and tenant workloads. NetworkPolicies restrict cross-namespace traffic. Istio AuthorizationPolicies enforce service-level boundaries. Platform namespaces protected from tenant access.

practices.title: Shared Resource Control
practices.implementation: ResourceQuotas and LimitRanges prevent resource exhaustion. Each tenant namespace has isolated resource quotas. Kubernetes scheduler ensures fair resource distribution.

practices.title: Public Access System Separation
practices.implementation: Istio gateway separates public-facing services from internal services. NetworkPolicies enforce isolation between public and internal namespaces. AuthorizationPolicies restrict access to internal APIs.

practices.title: Network Communication by Exception
practices.implementation: All namespaces start with default-deny NetworkPolicies. Network communication only allowed via explicit NetworkPolicy rules. Istio AuthorizationPolicies provide additional allow-listing at the service level.

practices.title: Split Tunneling
practices.implementation: Istio egress gateway can be configured to route all outbound traffic through a central point. NetworkPolicies restrict egress to approved destinations. Full split tunneling prevention requires additional network infrastructure controls.

practices.title: CUI Encryption in Transit
practices.implementation: Istio mTLS STRICT mode encrypts all in-cluster traffic with TLS 1.2+ and FIPS-compliant cipher suites. TLS termination at Istio gateway for external traffic. RKE2 control plane communication uses mTLS.

practices.title: Network Disconnect
practices.implementation: Keycloak session timeouts terminate inactive connections. SSH ClientAliveInterval terminates idle SSH sessions. Istio circuit breakers handle connection failures.

practices.title: Key Management
practices.implementation: cert-manager automates TLS certificate lifecycle. OpenBao manages secrets and PKI certificates with automatic rotation. Istio istiod manages workload certificates. FIPS 140-2 validated crypto modules used.

practices.title: CUI Encryption at Rest
practices.implementation: Kubernetes Secrets encrypted at rest via RKE2 default AES-CBC encryption. OpenBao data encrypted with seal key protected by KMS. Loki storage encrypted via S3 server-side encryption.

practices.title: Collaborative Device Control
practices.implementation: Collaborative computing device control is outside the scope of the SRE platform.

practices.title: Mobile Code
practices.implementation: Container image restrictions via Kyverno prevent unauthorized code execution. NeuVector process monitoring blocks unexpected executables. Mobile code in web UIs is managed per-application.

practices.title: Voice over IP
practices.implementation: VoIP is outside the scope of the SRE platform.

practices.title: Communications Authenticity
practices.implementation: Istio mTLS provides session authenticity via cryptographic mutual authentication with SPIFFE identities. TLS certificates managed by cert-manager ensure server authentication. Keycloak OIDC tokens prevent session hijacking.

practices.title: Data at Rest
practices.implementation: All persistent data encrypted via storage encryption. OpenBao seals data at rest with auto-unseal via KMS. Kubernetes Secrets encrypted by RKE2.

practices.title: Flaw Remediation
practices.implementation: Trivy scanning in Harbor with severity-based alerts. Flux automates image updates via GitOps. Ansible applies OS patches. NeuVector tracks runtime CVEs.

practices.title: Malicious Code Protection
practices.implementation: NeuVector runtime protection monitors processes, file systems, and network traffic. Behavioral learning creates baselines for anomaly detection. DLP sensors detect malicious patterns.

practices.title: Security Alerts
practices.implementation: Grafana alerting to Slack/email/PagerDuty. NeuVector CVE alerts. Prometheus AlertManager with configurable receivers and escalation policies.

practices.title: Update Malicious Code Protection
practices.implementation: NeuVector scanner definitions updated automatically. Trivy vulnerability database updated on each scan. Harbor replication keeps base images current from upstream sources.

practices.title: System and File Scanning
practices.implementation: NeuVector performs real-time file system monitoring and integrity checking on running containers. Harbor/Trivy scans all images on push. CIS benchmark scanning validates configurations.

practices.title: Monitor Communications
practices.implementation: Prometheus metrics, Loki logs, Tempo traces, NeuVector runtime events, and Kyverno policy reports provide comprehensive monitoring. Grafana provides unified visualization.

practices.title: Identify Unauthorized Use
practices.implementation: NeuVector behavioral monitoring identifies unauthorized processes and network connections. Kyverno reports unauthorized image sources. Kubernetes audit logs capture unauthorized API access attempts. Istio reports unauthorized service-to-service calls.
